title: linaria lineolata
intro: Linaria lineolata, also known as toadflax or butter and eggs, is a perennial plant species in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. In herbal preparations, Linaria lineolata is often used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as digestive issues, like diarrhea and indigestion. The plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
